mission: Exhaust the three residual charged Paper 35 routes left after canonical spin normalization: a non-neutral physical surface sector, a puncture-moduli-to-horizon determinant transfer, and hidden CP-odd inflow inside the standard U(1) Chern-Simons surface data.

theorem_1_nonneutral_surface_sector_not_.statement: The standard physical ABCK surface sector admits no non-neutral total U(1) charge. Constant diagonal U(1) transformations are pure gauge on the puncture phase space, and the quantum boundary condition restricts physical basis labels to weights a = (a_i) with Σ_i a_i = 0 mod k. Therefore any sector with nonzero total surface charge is not a physical state of the current stack but a genuinely new extension.

theorem_2_no_current_stack_transfer_to_h.statement: The current standard puncture data do not derive any transfer theorem from the theta-function line bundle over puncture phase space to an extra determinant line over the horizon sphere. The physical puncture characters are globally neutral, and the prequantum line bundle lives on X_V rather than S^2. After filling in the punctures, the only derived sphere bundle is the already-fixed canonical spin bundle Q. Hence the current-stack value of any extra transferred determinant degree is zero.

formulas.fill_in_output: only canonical Q is derived on S^2

imports.surface_boundary: The standard ABCK puncture sector is now exhausted for Paper 35. Its physical characters are globally neutral, and its quantization line bundle lives over the puncture phase-space torus rather than the horizon sphere. So it cannot secretly supply the residual spin^c integer n on the current stack.
imports.spin_c_boundary: The Paper 35 charged frontier is now exact after spin normalization. The fixed horizon spin bundle already contributes the canonical unit degree. No current-stack theorem produces any extra spin^c shift beyond it. So the only live positive route is a genuinely new residual spin^c class n ≠ 0, not a hidden completion of the existing carrier.

theorem_3_no_hidden_cp_odd_inflow_in_sta.statement: The standard U(1) Chern-Simons surface sector does not hide a CP-odd inflow theorem for the current fermionic bridge. Its role on the current stack is to quantize the puncture phase space and the discrete gauge action on H_s; it is not a derived 3D fermionic parity-odd action whose boundary variation shifts the eta invariant of the horizon Dirac operator. Combined with the closed-S^2 carrier and zero extra determinant twist, there is no hidden current-stack inflow source for nonzero Q5.

formulas.current_surface_cs_role: quantization of X_V and lift of Z_k gauge action
formulas.not_derived: no 3D fermionic parity-odd action with boundary eta-shift

imports.aps_boundary: APS/anomaly inflow is not a live route on the current stack. The admitted horizon carrier is a closed S^2 problem with no derived open boundary end and no derived 3D parity-odd Chern-Simons functional whose variation would generate a boundary eta-shift. So anomaly inflow is not currently hidden in the existing IO data; it would require a genuinely new 3D parity-odd boundary extension.

final_boundary.status: derived / exhausted current-stack Paper 35 charged-routes boundary
final_boundary.safe_statement: After canonical spin normalization, every remaining charged current-stack route is exhausted. There is no physical non-neutral surface sector, no derived transfer from puncture-moduli line data to an extra determinant line on S^2, and no hidden CP-odd inflow inside the standard surface Chern-Simons sector. The only positive future route is genuinely new charged boundary physics beyond the present stack.
